title: Finding Area by Counting Unit Squares

steps.instruction: Area measures how much surface a shape covers. We measure it in square units.
steps.explanation: Area answers the question: how many unit squares fit inside?

steps.instruction: A rectangle is covered with unit squares: 3 rows, 5 in each row.
steps.explanation: Count each row systematically to avoid missing any.
The area of this rectangle is 15 square units.

title: Area of an Irregular Shape by Counting

steps.instruction: The shape is not a perfect rectangle, but it's still covered with unit squares.
steps.work: The shape looks like a plus sign (+), made of unit squares.
steps.explanation: Even non-rectangular shapes have area. Just count all the unit squares.

steps.instruction: Count every unit square inside the shape, row by row.
steps.explanation: Go row by row so you don't miss any or count any twice.
Always include 'square units' when stating area.
